app_welcome_subtitle: Open a repository to get started
provider_description: Connect to your GitLab or GitHub instance with a Personal Access Token to access CI runs, merge requests, and more.
provider_instance_hint_github: Only fill this if you use a self-hosted GitHub Enterprise instance. Leave blank for github.com.
provider_instance_hint_gitlab: Only fill this if you use a self-hosted GitLab instance. Leave blank for gitlab.com.
provider_token_get_title_github: Generate a new token on GitHub
provider_token_get_title_gitlab: Generate a new token on GitLab
auth_github_scope_hint_workflow: To trigger, retry or cancel workflow runs, the token also needs the 'workflow' scope.
diff_empty: Select a file to view diff
graph_revert_confirm: Create a new commit that undoes the changes from {sha}?
graph_reset_confirm_soft: Move HEAD to {sha}. Your changes will remain staged.
graph_reset_confirm_mixed: Move HEAD to {sha}. Your changes will become unstaged.
graph_reset_confirm_hard: Move HEAD to {sha}. All uncommitted changes will be permanently lost.
graph_rebase_confirm: Rebase the current branch onto {sha}? Conflicts may occur.
pipeline_trigger_no_workflows: No workflows found in this repository
pipeline_no_provider: Connect a provider in Settings to view CI runs
pipeline_no_provider_description: Connect a GitHub or GitLab token in Settings to see workflow runs.
pipeline_no_runs_description: Push a commit to trigger your first CI run.
pipeline_no_match: No CI runs match the filter
pipeline_no_match_description: Adjust the status filter or search query.
pipeline_select_run: Select a CI run to view details
joblog_empty: Select a job to view its log
joblog_unavailable: Logs not yet available — retrying automatically
connection_howto_lead: BeardGit supports two authentication paths — a personal access token (PAT) entered below, or a pre-authenticated CLI (`gh` / `glab`) on your PATH. Most users only need one.
connection_howto_standard_description: If you work against the public instances, pick either method:
connection_howto_standard_token_body: Create a token in your profile and paste it below (section "Token Authentication").
connection_howto_standard_cli_body: Run the login flow once in any terminal; BeardGit reuses that session automatically:
connection_howto_selfhosted_description: Self-hosted instances need extra wiring. Pick OAuth if your admin has registered an application on the instance, otherwise use the token fallback.
connection_howto_selfhosted_oauth_body: Set the registered client_id for your host, then log in. Ask your GitLab admin for the client_id if you don't have it.
connection_howto_selfhosted_token_body: Generate a PAT on your self-hosted instance and hand it to `glab` directly:
connection_howto_selfhosted_token_scope_: Scopes: api, read_api, read_repository. PAT page: https://<your-host>/-/user_settings/personal_access_tokens.
connection_howto_troubleshoot_multi_conf: glab 1.9x reads from `~/.config/glab-cli/config.yml` and ignores the older `~/Library/Application Support/glab-cli/config.yml`. Compare them:
If your auth lives in the ignored file, copy the relevant host section into the active file (or merge both into one).
connection_howto_troubleshoot_404_body: Usually means glab is hitting gitlab.com instead of your self-hosted instance. Confirm your remote points at the right host (`git remote get-url origin`) and that the corresponding hostname is registered in `glab auth status`.
connection_howto_pat_intro: PAT is the recommended path — a successful connect also logs the matching CLI in automatically.
connection_howto_pat_token_type_warning: Do **not** use fine-grained tokens — they break `gh`/`glab` because they don't support the GraphQL API.
connection_howto_pat_sso_body: If your organisation uses SSO, click **Configure SSO → Authorize** next to your token on the PAT settings page before pasting it here.
connection_howto_pat_manual_body: If the automatic CLI login fails, run one of these in a terminal.
tasks_select_task: Select a task to view output
stash_select_preview: Select a stash to preview changes
tags_delete_body: Delete tag "{name}"? This removes it from your local repository.
tags_select_preview: Select a tag to view details
tags_create_commit_hint: Paste a full or abbreviated commit SHA
tags_create_help_name: Must be a valid git ref name (no spaces, no ~ ^ : ? * [ \ )
tags_create_help_target: Choose a branch tip or enter a specific commit SHA
tags_create_help_annotated: Annotated tags store author, date, and a message. Use for releases. Lightweight tags are just pointers.
conflict_resolved_all: All conflicts resolved — ready to continue
worktree_remove_confirm: Remove worktree at "{path}"? The directory will be deleted.
settings_remote_remove_confirm: Remove remote "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
settings_remote_no_repo: Open a repository to see its settings.
staging_discard_confirm: Discard selected changes? This cannot be undone.
blame_no_file: Select a file to view blame
file_history_click_to_blame: Click a commit to view blame at that point
merge_resolve_confirm_message: The file may still contain conflict markers (<<<<<<, ======, >>>>>>). Are you sure you want to mark it as resolved?
rebase_first_squash_error: The first commit can't be squashed or fixed up — there's no earlier commit to combine it into.
reflog_select_entry: Select a reflog entry to view details
changes_menu_delete_confirm_message: This will permanently delete "{path}". This cannot be undone.
changes_menu_discard_confirm_message: Discard unstaged changes in "{path}"? This cannot be undone.
clean_dialog_warning: This will permanently delete the selected files. This cannot be undone.
gitignore_editor_empty: No .gitignore file. Start typing to create one.
patch_no_changes: No changes to create a patch from
submodule_deinit_confirm: Deinitialize submodule "{name}"? Its working tree will be removed.
submodule_deinit_force_confirm: Force deinitialize submodule "{name}"? All local changes will be lost.
submodule_remove_confirm: Remove submodule "{name}"? This will deinitialize it and remove it from the index.
provider_login_oauth_desc: Opens your browser for secure OAuth authentication.
provider_cli_not_found: CLI tool not available. Use a Personal Access Token instead.
mrpr_no_provider: Connect a provider to view merge requests
mrpr_select: Select a merge request to view details
mrpr_select_github: Select a pull request to view details
mrpr_close_confirm: Close this merge request without merging?
mrpr_close_confirm_github: Close this pull request without merging?
mrpr_empty_no_changes: No changes in this pull request.
issues_empty_description: No issues match this repository's active filters.
issues_no_provider: Connect a provider to view issues
issues_no_provider_description: Add a GitHub or GitLab access token in Settings to browse issues.
issues_no_filter_description: Try removing search tags or reloading.
issues_select: Select an issue to view details
update_settings_auto_check_label: Automatically check for updates on startup
update_settings_auto_check_hint: When off, check manually with the button above.
update_reauth_title: One more click after the update
update_reauth_body_macos: macOS Gatekeeper will ask you to reopen BeardGit the first time you launch the new version — the same unsigned-app prompt you saw the first time you installed BeardGit. Just click "Open" in the dialog and you're back in.
update_reauth_body_windows: Windows SmartScreen will show its "Unrecognized app" warning the first time you launch the new version — the same prompt you saw the first time you installed BeardGit. Click "More info" then "Run anyway" to continue.
update_reauth_sorry: Sorry for the friction — once our Apple / Microsoft developer certificates are in place, this dialog goes away forever.
ai_no_staged_changes: No staged changes to generate a commit message
ai_commit_message_disabled_tooltip: You need to stage changes to generate a commit message
ai_review_disabled_tooltip: You need to stage changes to get a review
ai_settings_no_providers: No AI tools detected on this system
ai_config_no_claude_md: No CLAUDE.md found. Create one manually or run /init in Claude Code.
ai_config_discard_confirm: You have unsaved changes. Discard them?
ai_sessions_empty: No AI sessions for this project
ai_sessions_empty_active: No terminals running AI for this project
ai_sessions_empty_conversations: No conversations found for this project
ai_sessions_empty_hint: Start one from the terminal dropdown or AI action buttons
ai_sessions_resume_error: Failed to open terminal for session: {error}
ai_sessions_resume_not_supported: Resume not supported for this provider
ai_sessions_resume_warning: Spawns a new process — {provider} will fork the conversation.
bisect_inactive_hint: Select a known good and bad commit to start bisecting
release_list_empty_description: Publish a release on your forge to see it here.
release_detail_empty: Select a release to view details
release_empty_blank: No release notes or assets published for {tag}.
release_no_provider: Connect a provider to view releases
release_no_provider_description: Connect a provider token in Settings to list releases.
release_delete_asset_confirm: Delete asset '{name}'? This cannot be undone.
release_delete_confirm: Delete release '{tag}'? The underlying tag stays, but release notes and assets are lost.
release_drop_hint: Drop files to upload as release assets
ai_background_worktree_slug_hint: Folder name under the AI worktree root
ai_background_prompt_placeholder: Describe what you want the agent to do...
ai_background_prompt_combine_hint: Free-text prompt will be appended to the selected saved prompt.
ai_background_saved_prompt_empty: No saved prompts found in this project or user scope
ai_background_skill_empty: No skills found in this project or user scope
ai_background_validation_no_prompt: Enter a prompt or pick a saved prompt / skill.
ai_background_validation_slug_exists: A worktree with this name already exists.
ai_background_discard_confirm: Remove the worktree and branch created for this run?
ai_background_output_empty_hint: The provider exited without writing to stdout or stderr. Open the worktree terminal to inspect manually, or rerun the prompt with the auto-accept-permissions toggle enabled.
ai_background_report_refresh_tooltip: Re-read the report file from disk
ai_background_report_pending_hint: The run is still in flight. The AI was asked to drop a markdown summary at .beardgit/ai-reports/<session>.md when it finishes.
ai_background_report_missing_hint: The provider finished without writing the report file. Common causes: the model couldn't use a Write tool (rerun with auto-accept-permissions), or it ignored the instruction. Switch to Show transcript to inspect the raw output.
ai_background_tab_button_tooltip: Open an AI session — interactive terminal or background worktree run (Cmd+Shift+A)
settings_ai_worktree_root_hint: Directory where AI worktrees are created. Relative paths are resolved against each repository root. Default: .beardgit/ai-worktrees
settings_ai_concurrency_hint: Maximum number of AI runs that can execute at the same time. Extra runs are queued.
settings_ai_auto_accept_hint: When on, AI runs use the provider's permission-skip flag. The agent can edit any file under the worktree without confirmation — use with care.
settings_cat_editor_description: Preferences for the in-app file editor.
settings_editor_extensions_description: Toggle CodeMirror plugins used by the in-app file editor.
settings_editor_smart_description: Lightweight per-language helpers — no language server required.
settings_editor_snippets_description: Tab-completable templates for common patterns (e.g. `fn` → function skeleton in Rust).
settings_editor_keyword_completion_descr: Suggest the language's reserved words alongside buffer matches.
settings_editor_json_lint_description: Highlight syntax errors in `.json` files; extra checks for `package.json` and `tsconfig.json`.
settings_editor_color_picker_description: Click on a `#hex` or `rgb(…)` literal to edit it visually. Useful in CSS / SCSS.
settings_editor_behavior_description: Indentation, file-tree filtering, and large-file warnings.
settings_editor_respect_gitignore_descri: Off by default — files matched by .gitignore are still shown so you can edit untracked / build-output files. Turn on to hide them.
settings_editor_large_file_warning: Warn when opening files larger than
settings_cat_git_description: Local, global, and system Git configuration.
settings_cat_ai_description: AI provider selection and background runs.
settings_cat_advanced_description: Updates, diagnostics, caches, and experimental flags.
settings_advanced_log_directory_hint: Opens the BeardGit log folder in the system file manager.
settings_advanced_clear_cache_descriptio: Deletes cached graph layouts. They will be rebuilt on the next repo open.
settings_advanced_clear_cache_failed: Could not clear the layout cache
settings_integrations_token_description: Connect to GitHub or GitLab with a PAT.
settings_integrations_cli_description: Detected status for the gh and glab command-line tools.
settings_integrations_connections_descri: GitHub / GitLab accounts and the gh / glab command-line tools, one row per integration.
settings_general_diff_section_descriptio: How the side-by-side diff viewer renders changes.
settings_general_diff_show_whitespace_hi: Render spaces as · and tabs as → so whitespace-only edits are visible.
settings_general_diff_line_wrapping_hint: Soft-wrap long lines so the entire content stays visible. When off, diffs render with a horizontal scrollbar. Independent from the editor's own line-wrapping preference.
settings_git_section_description: Inline editor for local + global Git config.
settings_ai_background_section_descripti: Concurrency, worktree root, and permission defaults.
repo_config_unsaved_footer: You have unsaved changes in this section.
repo_config_not_supported: No repo settings available for this project.
repo_config_authenticate_body: Sign in to the forge CLI so BeardGit can read and write repository settings on your behalf.
sidebar_min_visible: At least one section must be visible.
theme_load_failed: Could not load theme. Using built-in defaults.
pr_diff_missing_shas: This pull request is missing its base/head commit info — try refreshing the list.
update_server_unreachable: Couldn't reach the update server. Check your connection or try again later.
init_repo_intro: {path} is not a git repository. Do you want to initialize it?
init_repo_create_remote_disabled_hint: Sign in to a provider in Settings
init_repo_url_format_hint: Expected: https://, http://, ssh://, git@host:..., or a local path.
init_repo_action_init_commit_push_existi: Initialize, commit & push to existing remote
init_repo_success_toast_existing: Initialized {name} and pushed to the existing remote
tooltip_add_remote: Wire an origin remote so you can push this repo somewhere.
tooltip_mode_create: Create a new repository on the selected forge in your namespace.
tooltip_mode_existing: Wire an already-existing remote URL as origin.
tooltip_provider: Which forge hosts the new repo.
tooltip_name: Repository name. Letters, digits, dot, dash, underscore. 1–100 chars.
tooltip_visibility_public: Anyone with the URL can see this repo.
tooltip_visibility_private: Only you and collaborators can see this repo.
tooltip_remote_url: Full clone URL — HTTPS, SSH, or git@ — of an empty existing remote.
tooltip_commit_files: Stage all current files and create the "Initial commit".
init_repo_step_wire_origin_new: Wire origin to the new clone URL
clone_dialog_intro: Paste the clone URL of the repo you want to fetch and pick where it should land.
clone_dialog_url_format_hint: Expected: https://, http://, ssh://, git@host:..., or a local path.
clone_dialog_error_destination_exists: {path} already exists. Pick a different parent folder, or remove the existing one.
tooltip_clone_url: Full clone URL — HTTPS, SSH, or git@ — of the repo you want to clone.
tooltip_clone_destination: Parent folder. The repo will be created as a subdirectory inside it.
editor_no_project_open: Open a project to start editing files.
editor_no_tab_open: Pick a file from the tree to start editing.
editor_tree_truncated: Listing truncated — refine the filter to see more.
editor_save_tooltip: Save the file. Hold Shift while clicking to also stage it for the next commit.
editor_save_and_stage_tooltip: Write the file to disk and stage it for the next commit. Release Shift to revert to a plain Save.
editor_external_change_body: {name} has been modified outside the editor.
editor_binary_file: This file is binary and cannot be edited here.
editor_path_exists: A file or folder with that name already exists.
